title: Gambling It All: The Cruz Family's Journey
content:
The Cruz family was exhausted. Two days before Christmas in 2024, Rachel, a public high school teacher, was battling bronchitis after finishing her 17th year. Her husband, Irvi, was sleeping after working grueling back-to-back shifts at a bistro and a Latin dance club. They were drained from years of hustling just to keep their family afloat in New York City. Now, they were preparing to make an impossible decision: leaving the United States entirely.

The family had never been apart for long. Separation was simply not an option, so they decided that all four of them, including their daughters Sara and Ana, would go together. Rachel confessed that they no longer had faith that things would improve for them in America. Irvi, though exhausted and scared, felt a sense of excitement about finally living freely for the first time in his adult life. However, it marked the bittersweet end of their identity as proud New Yorkers.

Irvi's journey in the United States had been defined by mixed messages and constant uncertainty. He had arrived in upstate New York as a 19-year-old just before September 11, 2001. As the government tightened immigration policies and created the Department of Homeland Security, Irvi’s employers continued to rely on his labor as if nothing had changed. After briefly returning to Mexico due to homesickness, he realized his home country offered few opportunities. He ultimately chose to build his life and family in America, never expecting that he would one day be forced to leave.
